{"step_by_step_thinking": "Respiratory distress syndrome (RDS) primarily affects preterm neonates, and term infants are less frequently affected. The onset of respiratory distress in a term newborn suggests a different etiology. Antenatal corticosteroids are known to reduce the incidence of RDS in preterm infants. An air bronchogram on chest x-ray is more indicative of meconium aspiration syndrome. The onset of distress after 6 hours of birth is more consistent with RDS in term infants. Therefore, the history of receiving antenatal corticosteroids would not favor RDS in a term newborn.", "answer_choice": "A"}
